name: Extension Manager
description: Manage Chatons extensions from the marketplace. Search, install, uninstall, and debug extensions with built-in tools.

tools.name: extension.search_marketplace
tools.label: Search Extension Marketplace
tools.description: Search for extensions available on the Chatons marketplace. Find extensions by name, category, or keywords to install new functionality.
tools.promptSnippet: Search the extension marketplace for available extensions.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user wants to find new extensions to install.
Provide clear search terms like 'linear', 'calendar', 'telegram', etc.
The search will return matching extensions with their descriptions and install status.

query.description: Search query to find extensions by name, description, or keywords.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 20).

tools.name: extension.list_installed
tools.label: List Installed Extensions
tools.description: List all currently installed extensions with their status, version, and health information.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user wants to see what extensions are currently installed.
This shows both enabled and disabled extensions.

tools.name: extension.install
tools.label: Install Extension
tools.description: Install an extension from the marketplace by its npm package name. Extensions are downloaded from npm and installed locally.
tools.promptSnippet: Install an extension from the marketplace.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user asks to install an extension.
Always provide the full npm package name (e.g., '@thibautrey/chatons-extension-linear').
Search the marketplace first if the user doesn't know the exact package name.

id.description: The npm package name of the extension to install (e.g., '@thibautrey/chatons-extension-linear').

tools.name: extension.uninstall
tools.label: Uninstall Extension
tools.description: Remove an installed extension completely. This deletes the extension files and removes it from the registry.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user asks to remove or uninstall an extension.
Always provide the exact extension ID.
List installed extensions first if the user is unsure of the exact ID.

id.description: The ID of the extension to uninstall (e.g., '@thibautrey/chatons-extension-linear').

tools.name: extension.get_logs
tools.label: Get Extension Logs
tools.description: Read the runtime and installation logs of an extension for debugging purposes. Useful when an extension is not working correctly.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user reports an extension is not working or showing errors.
Always provide the exact extension ID.
Logs include both runtime logs and installation logs if available.

id.description: The ID of the extension to get logs for.

lines.description: Maximum number of log lines to return (default 500).

tools.name: extension.check_updates
tools.label: Check for Extension Updates
tools.description: Check if any installed extensions have updates available in the marketplace.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user wants to know if their extensions are up to date.
Returns a list of extensions that can be updated with their current and latest versions.

tools.name: extension.update
tools.label: Update Extension
tools.description: Update an installed extension to the latest version available in the marketplace.
tools.promptSnippet: Update an extension to the latest version.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user asks to update a specific extension.
Check for updates first if the user is unsure which extensions need updating.
Always provide the exact extension ID.

id.description: The ID of the extension to update.

tools.name: extension.toggle
tools.label: Enable/Disable Extension
tools.description: Enable or disable an installed extension without uninstalling it. Disabled extensions won't be loaded or show their UI.
promptGuidelines: Use this tool when the user wants to temporarily disable an extension without removing it.
Or when they want to re-enable a previously disabled extension.
Always provide the exact extension ID and the desired state (true for enabled, false for disabled).

id.description: The ID of the extension to enable or disable.

enabled.description: true to enable the extension, false to disable it.
